message:
fix(agentmail,checkpoint,health): agent registry remap on re-register, compressed scrollback restore, rate-limit restart guard

Agent registry (session.go, session_test.go):
- Fix AddAgent() to clean up stale mappings when an agent re-registers
  with a new pane title or pane ID — previously, moving an agent to a
  new tmux pane left orphaned entries in both Agents and PaneIDMap maps,
  causing lookup inconsistencies
- Guard against empty paneTitle to prevent overwriting valid mappings
- Add TestSessionAgentRegistry_AddAgent_RefreshesMappingsForSameAgent
  verifying old title/ID mappings are removed and count stays at 1

Spawn (spawn.go):
- Call registry.AddAgent() when reusing an existing agent name during
  spawn, ensuring the registry stays current if the pane was recreated

Checkpoint restore (restore.go, restore_test.go):
- Switch from LoadScrollback to LoadCompressedScrollback for pane
  context injection, supporting gzip-compressed scrollback files that
  reduce checkpoint storage by ~70%
- Use tmux.SendBuffer instead of tmux.SendKeys for context injection
  to handle large scrollback content without tmux input buffering issues
- Extract loadPaneScrollback helper for testability
- Add TestRestorer_loadPaneScrollback_Compressed validating round-trip
  through gzipCompress → SaveCompressedScrollback → loadPaneScrollback

Health auto-restart (health.go, health_auto_restart_test.go):
- Extract shouldAutoRestartHealthState() from AutoRestartUnhealthyAgent
  to make restart decision logic independently testable
- Change rate-limited agents from triggering restart to returning a
  "waiting for cooldown" reason, preventing restart loops during
  provider rate limiting
- Add TestShouldAutoRestartHealthState with 4 cases: unhealthy (restart),
  rate_limited (wait), healthy (skip), degraded (skip)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2553,6 +2553,7 @@ func registerSpawnedAgents(workingDir, sessionName string, agents []spawnedAgent
 				legacyPath := legacyIdentityFilePath(workingDir, agent.paneID)
 				_ = os.WriteFile(legacyPath, []byte(existingName+"\n"), 0o600)
 			}
+			registry.AddAgent(agent.paneTitle, agent.paneID, existingName)
 			continue
 		}
